chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: Next we also have the slicing operation.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: We have already seen slicing in the list.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: Just like there is slicing in the list, we can perform slicing operation on numpy arrays.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: So let's take an example of a 1D array for the slicing operation.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: Now to slice, we know what is the format of our slice.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: First we pass our starting index, then we pass our ending index which is not included.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: And after that we pass our step.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: Step is basically how much we want to skip.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: So we can actually do our slicing on an array.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: Normally we slice in the same way as we slice on a list.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: For example, we want to start with the first index and we want to end at the third index.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: So in this way we will get the values.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: First index means we started from 2, this is second index, third index.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: We will stop before the fourth index.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: So it will be 2, 3, 4.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: Our start and end is optional.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: If we don't define any ending value, then the entire array comes to us from the starting.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: If we don't define any starting value, then all the values come to us from the zeroth index.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: If we want, we can't define anything.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: So in that case, we get the entire array.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: And if we want, we can define the step.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: Step means how many elements we want to skip.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: We want all the alternate elements, then we are going to get the alternate elements like this.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: So this is how slicing works.

chunks.title: Slicing
chunks.text: Very similar to lists.

text: Next we also have the slicing operation. We have already seen slicing in the list. Just like there is slicing in the list, we can perform slicing operation on numpy arrays. So let's take an example of a 1D array for the slicing operation. Now to slice, we know what is the format of our slice. First we pass our starting index, then we pass our ending index which is not included. And after that we pass our step. Step is basically how much we want to skip. So we can actually do our slicing on an array. Normally we slice in the same way as we slice on a list. For example, we want to start with the first index and we want to end at the third index. So in this way we will get the values. First index means we started from 2, this is second index, third index. We will stop before the fourth index. So it will be 2, 3, 4. Our start and end is optional. If we don't define any ending value, then the entire array comes to us from the starting. If we don't define any starting value, then all the values come to us from the zeroth index. If we want, we can't define anything. So in that case, we get the entire array. And if we want, we can define the step. Step means how many elements we want to skip. We want all the alternate elements, then we are going to get the alternate elements like this. So this is how slicing works. Very similar to lists.
